rulings.Case Title: JULIA MALDONADO VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of The Issues
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 24, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of The Issues. Off calendar per 11/12/24 request for dismissal of entire action. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARSHALL HOLLAND VS. THE MOSSER COMPANIES, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Mosser Companies, Inc.?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 24, 2025 Line 9. DEFENDANTs MOSSER COMPANIES, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant the Mosser Companies, Inc.'s "motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories, set one and request for monetary sanctions" is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL HENRY VS. ERIC YOUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 24, 2025 Line 3. DEFENDANT ANDREW LEWIS' Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.7. Defendant Andrew Smith Lewis's motion for sanctions under CCP 128.7 is DENIED. The court has reviewed the record. The court does not find that Plaintiff's "action against Smith Lewis," construed as each and every allegation and claim made as to Lewis and every statement made about Lewis, is entirely lacking in evidentiary support, completely without support in law or presented primarily for an improper purpose. (See Notice of Mot. at p. 1; Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7(b).) Nor are Smith's more discrete arguments well taken on this record. The motion is, thus, denied. Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is denied, as well. The request is not properly before the court. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: LYDIA SEEBACH WENTZ ET AL VS. SEVEN SEAS MUSIC, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 24, 2025 Line 4. PLAINTIFFs LYDIA WENTZ, and SCOTT WENTZ' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Amend Complaint. Plaintiffs Lydia Wentz and Scott Wentz's Motion for Leave to Amend Complaint is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Plaintiffs are granted leave to file the proposed Verified First Amended Complaint. The court will not file the new complaint for the parties. Plaintiffs must file a Verified First Amended Complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit B to the Barari Declaration, presenting a copy of this order at the time of filing, within ten days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: BARCLAYS BANK DELAWARE VS. YVES H BOUNGOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6)Points And Authorities; And Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 24, 2025 Line 5. PLAINTIFF BARCLAYS BANK's Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6). Plaintiff Barclays Bank Delaware's unopposed Motion To Enforce Settlement (C.C.P. Section 664.6) is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered in favor of Plaintiff and against Defendant Yves H. Boungou for the sum of $1,518.18, plus costs of $479.20, for a total of $1,997.38. Plaintiff is admonished to lodge a proposed order and proposed judgment when presenting this type of request to the court and to provide courtesy copies of all papers, including proposed orders and judgments, to the Department. Failure to do so will likely result in the motion being taken off calendar. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: SHAKAR HERSHKOVITZ VS. UBER TECHNOLGOIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismissal Or In The Alternative, Stay The Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 24, 2025 Line 7. DEFENDANTs UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC.,and RASIER-CA LLC.'s Motion To Dismissal Or In The Alternative, Stay The Action. Specially Appearing Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier LLC, Rasier-CA, LLC's Motion To Dismissal [sic] Or In The Alternative, Stay The Action Based On Forum Non Conveniens is GRANTED subject to the condition that Defendants stipulate to toll the applicable statute of limitations in India beginning from the date Plaintiff filed this action and ending on the date of this order. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person.
